factors.summary: Economic conditions, particularly in the oil and commodities sectors, are negatively impacting demand.

sentiment.rationale: Negative impact on demand from low oil and commodities prices.

factors.summary: Free cash flow decreased due to lower net earnings and changes in working capital.

evidence.text: The $112.3 million decrease in cash flows from operating activities during the three months ended January 1, 2016 as compared to the corresponding period last year was due primarily to a $59.8 million decrease in cash generated from changes within our working capital accounts (discussed below) and a $55.9 million decrease in Group net earnings.
evidence.subsection: Table of Contents JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Decrease in free cash flow due to lower net earnings and working capital changes.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards lower-margin segments, particularly due to reduced pass-through costs and lower volumes in certain markets.

evidence.text: The decrease in revenues was due primarily to reduced pass-through costs included in revenues combined with lower volumes in the Chemicals and Polymers, National Government, Mining and Minerals, and Oil & Gas - Upstream markets. Also contributing to the decrease in revenues in the first quarter of fiscal 2016 as compared to the corresponding period last year was effects of the strengthening U.S. dollar.
evidence.subsection: Table of Contents JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Shift towards lower-margin segments and reduced volumes affecting revenue.

factors.summary: End markets vary in momentum: Pharmaceuticals and Biotechnology are growing, while Chemicals and Polymers, Oil & Gas-Upstream, and Mining and Minerals are slowing.

evidence.text: For the three months ended January 1, 2016, revenues from clients operating in the Pharmaceuticals and Biotechnology market increased $30.5 million, or 23.5%, to $160.3 million from $129.8 million for the corresponding period last year. Revenues from clients operating in the Mining and Minerals market decreased $72.0 million, or 50.7%, to $70.0 million from $142.1 million for the corresponding period last year.
evidence.subsection: Table of Contents JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

factors.summary: Input costs are stable, but lower commodity prices are affecting client spending rather than directly impacting the company's costs.

evidence.text: The effects of lower prices of crude oil and certain commodities continue to put pressure on the capital spending plans of our clients operating in the Chemicals & Polymers, Oil & Gas-Upstream, and Mining and Minerals industries and markets.
evidence.subsection: Table of Contents JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Stable input costs but external pressures on client spending.

factors.summary: Labor costs are manageable, with restructuring actions helping to lower the overall cost structure.

evidence.text: The Company's 2015 Restructuring was implemented to improve operational efficiency, reduce costs, and better position itself to drive growth. The costs of the 2015 Restructuring are included in selling, general, and administrative expense in the accompanying Consolidated Statements of Earnings for the first quarter of fiscal 2016.

sentiment.label: positive

factors.summary: Restructuring actions are delivering measurable savings, with costs incurred during the first quarter of fiscal 2016.

evidence.text: The Company's consolidated results of operations for the first quarter of fiscal 2016 include $68.4 million pre-tax costs associated with the 2015 Restructuring. The costs of the 2015 Restructuring were included in selling, general, and administrative expense in the accompanying Consolidated Statements of Earnings for the first quarter of fiscal 2016.
evidence.subsection: Table of Contents JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

sentiment.label: positive

factors.summary: Long-term strategic direction includes focusing on higher-margin services like FEED and pre-FEED, and shifting towards sustaining capital projects in mining and minerals.

evidence.text: We have long-term relationships with numerous chemical companies around the world, and we continue to believe this industry group will provide strong growth opportunities in the long-term. The Company is refocusing and reallocating its resources onto brownfield and sustaining capital-type projects. Longer-term, we believe we are well positioned to realize incremental business when the current pricing environment improves.

factors.summary: The backlog orders decreased by 4.6% to $18.2 billion, primarily due to lower new awards and unfavorable market conditions, including the impact of foreign exchange. Despite a stable backlog on a constant currency basis, new orders were insufficient to offset work-off, project cancellations, and reductions in scope, particularly affecting industries like petroleum, chemicals, mining, and minerals due to low crude oil prices and commodity challenges.

evidence.text: Our backlog decreased $0.9 billion , or 4.6% , to $18.2 billion at January 1, 2016 from $19.1 billion at December 26, 2014 . Of the $0.9 billion decrease, approximately $470.0 million is due to the effects of foreign exchange. Although backlog at January 1, 2016 includes new awards from clients operating in many of the industry groups and markets we serve, these awards were insufficient to off-set the effects of work-off, project cancellations, and reductions in scope. In addition, backlog has been further challenged as it relates to clients operating in the petroleum, chemicals, and mining and minerals industries due to the effects of the continuing low crude oil prices and certain commodities; i.e., copper and iron ore.
evidence.subsection: Table of Contents JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

evidence.text: Backlog at January 1, 2016 was $18.2 billion, and represents a decrease of 4.6% over backlog at December 26, 2014. On a constant currency basis, backlog was down 2.2%
evidence.subsection: Table of Contents JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Backlog orders have decreased significantly, primarily due to unfavorable market conditions and low demand in key industries.

factors.summary: The margins are facing downward pressure primarily due to a decrease in net earnings and foreign exchange effects. While gross margins remain stable, operating margins may come under additional pressure from restructuring costs.

evidence.text: The $112.3 million decrease in cash flows from operating activities during the three months ended January 1, 2016 as compared to the corresponding period last year was due primarily to a $59.8 million decrease in cash generated from changes within our working capital accounts (discussed below) and a $55.9 million decrease in Group net earnings.
evidence.subsection: Table of Contents JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

evidence.text: As a percentage of revenues, direct costs of contracts for the three months ended January 1, 2016 and December 26, 2014 was 84.5% and 83.7%, respectively. The relationship between direct costs of contracts and revenues will fluctuate between reporting periods depending on the mix of business during the reporting periods being compared as well as the level of margins earned from the various types of services provided.
evidence.subsection: Table of Contents JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Margins are under downward pressure due to decreased net earnings and foreign exchange effects, with potential additional pressure from restructuring.
